mnemonics: Markov Chains Move Stepwise: Memoryless property means the future state depends only on the current state, not the entire past history.
Probability Pathways: The transition matrix P has entries P_ij representing the probability of moving from state i to state j.
keyPoints: A Markov Chain is a stochastic model describing a sequence of possible events where the probability of each event depends only on the state attained in the previous event (the Markov property).
The system is defined by its state space and a transition probability matrix, where each entry P(i,j) is the probability of moving from state i to state j.
Steady-state distribution represents the long-term proportion of time the chain spends in each state, found by solving π = πP.
commonMistakes: Assuming the Markov property applies to all stochastic processes; it is specific to those with no memory beyond the current state.
Confusing the transition matrix with the steady-state vector; the transition matrix describes one-step probabilities, while the steady-state vector describes long-term behavior.
Forgetting to check if the Markov chain is irreducible and aperiodic when trying to find a unique steady-state distribution, as these properties are necessary for its existence.
